title: EMb encystment medium preparation (500 mL)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"39bu1","text":"Recipe for EMb encystment medium, which is used to induce synchronous encystment of Acanthamoeba castellanii trophozoites. Adapted from Neff protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":84,"length":24}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ak2nk","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"3u15","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"citation","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"496096F8341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","title":"Induction of synchronous encystment in Acanthamoeba spp","protocol_guid":"","journal":"Methods in cell Physiology volume 1 (Prescott DM, ed). Academic Press, New York; 1964","date":null,"created_on":"2026-02-10T22:33:37Z","doi":"","authors":"Neff R, Ray S, Benton W, Wilborn M","can_edit":false}}}}
steps: To 500 mL bottle, add: 3.728 g KCl (0.1 M) 1.680 g NaHCO 3 (0.04 M) 0.986 g MgSO 4 x 7H 2 O (8 mM) 0.030 g CaCl 2 x 2H 2 O (0.4 mM) 0.017 g 2-Amino-2-methyl-1,3-propanediol (AMPD, 0.32 mM) -stir bar We have found that AMPD is a superior amine buffer to Tris for this medium and maintains a constant pH over time. When Tris is used instead, the pH of the medium steadily decreases to 7 during storage, resulting in poor induction of encystment.
Bring volume to 500 mL with dH 2 O and place on stir plate (unheated).
Measure pH using pH meter. Confirm that the pH is between 8 and 8.5--anything in this range works for encystment.
Filter sterilize into sterile 500-mL plastic bottle using 0.22 µm filtration system. Storage in plastic, not glass, is essential to maintaining the pH.
creator: Carrie A. Flynn
